title: Login endpoint issues httpOnly session cookie
description: Implement the `auth.login` tRPC mutation that validates email/password against `users`, creates a row in `sessions`, and sets an httpOnly session cookie on the response.
acceptanceCriteria: `auth.login` mutation accepts `{ email, password }` and returns `{ userId, role }` on success
On success, a `sessions` row is created with `user_id` and `expira_em` (e.g. now + 7 days)
On success, response sets a `Set-Cookie` header with the session id, `httpOnly: true`, `sameSite: 'lax'`, `secure` in production
Invalid email or password returns a generic `UNAUTHORIZED` error without revealing which field was wrong
Password comparison uses `verifyPassword` from TASK-9, never plain string comparison
Successful login does not include `senha_hash` anywhere in the response payload

steps.description: Create auth router with login procedure
steps.details: Create `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/auth.ts` with a `login` mutation accepting a zod schema `{ email: z.string().email(), password: z.string().min(1) }`.

steps.description: Look up user and verify password
steps.details: Query `users` by `email`. If not found, throw `TRPCError({ code: 'UNAUTHORIZED', message: 'Invalid credentials' })`. If found, call `verifyPassword(password, user.senha_hash)`; if false, throw the same generic error.
On successful verification, insert into `sessions` a new row with a random session id (`crypto.randomUUID()`), `user_id`, and `expira_em` set 7 days out.
Using the tRPC HTTP adapter's response object, set `Set-Cookie: session_id=<id>; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax; Path=/; Max-Age=604800` (add `Secure` when `NODE_ENV=production`).

steps.description: Return user role, not the full user object
steps.details: Join `memberships` to fetch `role` for the authenticated user id, and return `{ userId, role }` â€” never `senha_hash`.

steps.description: Test the happy and unhappy paths
steps.details: Test: correct credentials return 200 with `Set-Cookie` present and a `sessions` row created; wrong password and unknown email both return the same `UNAUTHORIZED` error message and status.

technicalNotes: Using the same generic error message for 'user not found' and 'wrong password' prevents user enumeration.
Session id itself acts as the bearer credential in the cookie â€” no separate JWT is needed for this MVP's cookie-session model.
